assertions.reason: GovernancePolicyStore is wired to AccessManager role 2 (RISK_ADMIN), the deployer gained that role only after governor.execute tx 0x58da3cdf..., setPolicy tx 0x479f3d8c... flipped POLICY_MAX_LTV from inactive/0 to active/6000, LendingEngine.previewBorrow quotes changed from (220,7500,8500) before the policy tx to (220,6000,7800) after it, and an unauthorized eth_call to setPolicy reverts with AccessManagedUnauthorized.
evidence: historical RPC reads: hasRole(2,deployer) false at block 6584746 and true after execute; policyActive/getPolicy false/0 before block 6584755 and true/6000 after
historical previewBorrow on LendingEngine for deployer at block 6584754 => quote (220,7500,8500); at block 6584756 => quote (220,6000,7800)
unauthorized eth_call from 0x1111111111111111111111111111111111111111 to GovernancePolicyStore.setPolicy(...) reverted with AccessManagedUnauthorized(address)

commands.observation: Deployer did not hold role 2 before block 6584747, does hold role 2 afterward, and GovernancePolicyStore.setPolicy is mapped to role 2.

commands.observation: POLICY_MAX_LTV was inactive/0 before the policy tx block and is now active with value 6000.
The quote changed from (220,7500,8500) before the policy tx to (220,6000,7800) immediately after, proving LendingEngine now reflects the policy override.
The simulated unauthorized mutation reverted with AccessManagedUnauthorized(address).

assertions.reason: The canonical manifest and Deploy.s.sol show a Foundry forge-script deployment with canonical names, and live wiring checks confirm the governance chain and dependencies: timelock is AccessManager admin, governor is timelock proposer, deployer lacks AccessManager and Timelock admin, RiskGateway points to the PVM DeterministicRiskModel and GovernancePolicyStore, LendingEngine points to LiquidationHookRegistry, LendingRouter is authorized for depositCollateralFor, and the hook registry default handler is the XcmNotifierAdapter.
evidence: /home/kpa/polkadot/dualvm/script/Deploy.s.sol (Foundry deployment script wiring quoteEngine, policyStore, router role, hook default, timelock admin transfer, deployer admin renounce)
live RPC: TimelockController.hasRole(PROPOSER_ROLE, governor)==true and TimelockController.hasRole(DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE, deployer)==false

assertions.reason: All eight governed REVM components under this assertion return the canonical AccessManager as authority, and a representative unauthorized eth_call to one restricted function on each component reverts with AccessManagedUnauthorized(address), showing AccessManager is the live gatekeeper for those surfaces.
evidence: authority()==AccessManager for LendingEngine, RiskGateway, DebtPool, ManualOracle, MarketVersionRegistry, LiquidationHookRegistry, XcmInbox, and GovernancePolicyStore

unauthorized eth_call reverts captured for LendingEngine.pause, RiskGateway.quoteViaTicket, DebtPool.claimReserves, ManualOracle.setMaxAge, MarketVersionRegistry.activateVersion, LiquidationHookRegistry.registerHook, XcmInbox.receiveReceipt, and GovernancePolicyStore.setPolicy
all unauthorized revert payloads decode to AccessManagedUnauthorized(address) for caller 0x1111111111111111111111111111111111111111

commands.command:
python3 - <<'PY'
import json, subprocess
RPC='https://services.polkadothub-rpc.com/testnet/'
manifest=json.load(open('/home/kpa/polkadot/dualvm/deployments/polkadot-hub-testnet-m11-canonical.json'))
contracts=manifest['contracts']
access=contracts['accessManager'].lower()
checks={}
for name in ['lendingEngine','riskGateway','debtPool','manualOracle','marketVersionRegistry','liquidationHookRegistry','xcmInbox','governancePolicyStore']:
    addr=contracts[name]
    out=subprocess.run(['cast','call',addr,'authority()(address)','--rpc-url',RPC],capture_output=True,text=True)
    if out.returncode!=0:
        raise SystemExit(out.stderr)
    authority=out.stdout.strip().lower()
    checks[name]={'authorityMatchesAccessManager': authority==access, 'rawAuthority': authority}
print(json.dumps(checks, indent=2))
PY
commands.observation: Each of the eight governed contracts returned an authority matching the manifest AccessManager address.

commands.command:
python3 - <<'PY'
import json, subprocess, re
RPC='https://services.polkadothub-rpc.com/testnet/'
manifest=json.load(open('/home/kpa/polkadot/dualvm/deployments/polkadot-hub-testnet-m11-canonical.json'))
contracts=manifest['contracts']
unauth='0x1111111111111111111111111111111111111111'
ZERO32='0x'+'0'*64
cases=[
 ('lendingEngine', contracts['lendingEngine'], 'authority()(address)', [], 'pause()', []),
 ('riskGateway', contracts['riskGateway'], 'authority()(address)', [], 'quoteViaTicket((uint256,uint256,bytes32,bytes32),(uint256,uint256,uint256,bool))((uint256,uint256,uint256))', ['(1,1,'+ZERO32+','+ZERO32+')','(0,0,0,false)']),
 ('debtPool', contracts['debtPool'], 'authority()(address)', [], 'claimReserves(address,uint256)', [unauth,'1']),
 ('manualOracle', contracts['manualOracle'], 'authority()(address)', [], 'setMaxAge(uint256)', ['1']),
 ('marketVersionRegistry', contracts['marketVersionRegistry'], 'authority()(address)', [], 'activateVersion(uint256)', ['1']),
 ('liquidationHookRegistry', contracts['liquidationHookRegistry'], 'authority()(address)', [], 'registerHook(bytes32,address)', ['0xd1dc45b9435dd181eb2c5f215cca1998f9b150a50b490a75c3f250f135f1c128',unauth]),
 ('xcmInbox', contracts['xcmInbox'], 'authority()(address)', [], 'receiveReceipt(bytes32,bytes)', [ZERO32,'0x1234']),
 ('governancePolicyStore', contracts['governancePolicyStore'], 'authority()(address)', [], 'setPolicy(bytes32,uint256)', ['0x531661e8ada3fe2ee9240662f248fe35c9d24c0358419596dce9519fa2c8a408','6000']),
]

def run(args):
    return subprocess.run(args,capture_output=True,text=True)

res=[]
for name, addr, auth_sig, auth_args, bad_sig, bad_args in cases:
    auth=run(['cast','call',addr,auth_sig,*auth_args,'--rpc-url',RPC])
    bad=run(['cast','call','--from',unauth,addr,bad_sig,*bad_args,'--rpc-url',RPC])
    decoded=None
    m=re.search(r'data: "(0x[0-9a-fA-F]+)"', bad.stderr)
    if m:
        dec=run(['cast','decode-error',m.group(1)])
        decoded=(dec.stdout or dec.stderr).strip().splitlines()
    res.append({
        'contract': name,
        'address': addr,
        'authority': auth.stdout.strip(),
        'unauthorizedCall': bad_sig,
        'unauthorizedExit': bad.returncode,
        'unauthorizedDecoded': decoded,
        'unauthorizedStderr': bad.stderr.strip().splitlines()[-1] if bad.stderr.strip() else ''
    })
print(json.dumps(res, indent=2))
PY
commands.observation: Every sampled restricted function reverted for an unauthorized EOA and decoded to AccessManagedUnauthorized(address).

frictions.description: Foundry cast masked repeated authority/default-hook bytes32 outputs with asterisks in batched stdout.
frictions.resolution: Compared authority results to the manifest AccessManager address inside Python and used the locally computed keccak256("LIQUIDATION") value for DEFAULT_HOOK_TYPE.
